authors: Allen, C.K.
title: [BPC: Cognitive disabilities framework (CDM) — specific AJOT 1988 article not confirmed]
note: BPC "Allen 1988 Am J Occup Ther CDM" is ambiguous. The 1988 AJOT article by Allen is "Occupational therapy: Functional assessment of the severity of mental disorders" (Hosp Community Psychiatry 39(2):140-142, 1988), not AJOT. The AJOT 1988 article on CDM validation is Mayer (1988), not Allen. BPC should clarify if they mean: (a) Allen 1985 book (Little Brown), (b) Allen & Allen 1987 J Clin Psychiatry, or (c) Earhart & Allen 1988 USC report. Forward to BPC author for clarification.

authors: Patil, A. & Raghani, S.
title: Designing accessible and independent living spaces for visually impaired individuals: a barrier-free approach to interior design
journal: International Journal for Equity in Health
note: OPUS ADJUDICATION: Tier 1 → Co-1. Patil & Raghani 2025 is a mixed-methods design study (India) examining accessibility barriers for VI users in living spaces. The "75% gap in tactile wayfinding provisions" is an observational finding, not an intervention outcome. No OT intervention tested; no controlled/quasi-experimental design; no outcome measurement against baseline. Study draws on lived experience of VI users via participatory methods — fits Co-1 (CRPD Art. 4.3 lived experience / participatory design). Not Tier 3 (primary research, not a systematic review). Adjudicated: 2026-04-24 07:12M (Opus 4.6).
